name: Review notification (check your answers)
pageTitle: Review Notification - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
caption: DRAFT.GB.2026.1525979 - CHEDP
heading: Review your notification
bodyCopy: You must use the correct reference and code on the customs declaration or your consignment will be delayed.
layout: govuk-grid-row > govuk-grid-column-full (with govuk-grid-column-one-half used inside the goods/description block)

govukComponents.usage: H1 'Review your notification' plus H2/H3 section headings throughout the check-your-answers page

the two per-certificate captions 'Catch certificate 1 of 2' and 'Catch certificate 2 of 2' (only two .govuk-caption-m elements exist on the page). CORRECTION: the 'DRAFT.GB.2026.1525979 - CHEDP' reference banner is NOT a govuk-caption-m and is NOT above the H1 — it is a span.heading-tertiary in the page header (div.govuk-!-padding-top-6) above <main>. See nonStandardPatterns 'heading-tertiary reference banner'.
govukComponents.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 170 (querySelectorAll('.govuk-caption-m') => ['Catch certificate 1 of 2','Catch certificate 2 of 2']; #reference-number is span.heading-tertiary inside div.govuk-!-padding-top-6 in the header, before the <main> element)

govukComponents.usage: customs-reference warning: 'You must use the correct reference and code on the customs declaration or your consignment will be delayed.'
reference block (CHED reference / customs declaration reference / customs document code) and the 'About the consignment', Traders and Contact detail groups; each row can carry a Change link

catch-certificate summary table, per-certificate commodity tables, latest health certificate table, additional documents table, approved establishment, transport (before/after BCP), nominated contacts, and the species/weight commodity breakdown
primary 'Save and continue' submit; secondary/link-button variants used for Copy and Change actions
visible horizontal dividers between the reference/warning block, the certificate detail, and further review sections
'Change' links per review row and attachment (filename) download links
paragraph copy incl. 'Not Submitted' status and 'No exemptions specified'

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: review-summary-list, review-summary-list__row, review-summary-list__key, review-summary-list__value, review-summary-list__action, review-summary-list__row-border-bottom, review-summary-list__row-border-top, review-summary-list__row-no-action, review-summary-list__row-with-action
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: a bespoke IPAFFS re-skin of the GOV.UK summary list used for the check-your-answers rows, with its own border and action-column modifiers
nonStandardPatterns.concern: parallel component to govuk-summary-list; the rebuild should use the standard Summary list with a boolean per row for whether a Change action shows, rather than a custom class family
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 170 (main non-govuk class inventory)

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: presentation-table, presentation-table__row--no-border, presentation-table__section, review-table, review-additional-documents-table, review-latest-health-certificate-table, table-responsive
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: custom table variants for the commodity/species weight breakdown and the document/certificate summary tables, plus a responsive-table wrapper
nonStandardPatterns.concern: IPAFFS-specific table styling layered on top of (or instead of) govuk-table; the catch-certificate and health-certificate tables are load-bearing IUU data and should be rebuilt as standard GOV.UK Tables

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: renders the 'Last updated <date>' / 'by <user>' audit metadata block under the H1
nonStandardPatterns.concern: bespoke widget with no Design System equivalent; can be plain body paragraphs in the rebuild

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: the 'Copy' buttons beside the CHED reference / customs declaration reference / customs document code that copy the value to the clipboard, and link-styled action buttons
nonStandardPatterns.concern: requires client-side JS clipboard behaviour; a govuk-button styled as a link is standard, but the copy-to-clipboard interaction is custom and must be re-decided (progressive-enhancement) in the rebuild
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Button styled as a link (govuk-button--secondary or a govuk-link) plus bespoke clipboard JS
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 170 (refs e71, e76, e81)

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: lays a section heading and its 'Change' link on the same row
nonStandardPatterns.concern: layout helper; achievable with GOV.UK grid/utility classes in the rebuild

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: renders the '<CHED reference> - <CHED type>' banner ('DRAFT.GB.2026.1525979 - CHEDP') in the page header ABOVE <main>, wrapping the reference in span#reference-number and appending ' - CHEDP'
nonStandardPatterns.concern: bespoke header widget, not a GOV.UK Caption; the reviewer of an earlier draft mislabelled it as a govuk-caption-m Caption above the H1. For the IUU rebuild this reference/CHED-type identifier is a product decision (whether to surface it and how)
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-caption-l/-xl caption paired with the H1, or plain govuk-body
nonStandardPatterns.evidence:
trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 170 (#reference-number.closest('div') =
DRAFT.GB.2026.1525979 - CHEDP
, located before
)

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: no-wrap, ellipsis, break-word, border-bottom-none, consignment-net-weight, species, weight, packages, package-type, change
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: utility/formatting classes controlling text wrapping, truncation and column widths in the review tables
nonStandardPatterns.concern: custom CSS utilities; GOV.UK width-override utilities (govuk-!-width-*) already cover most of this and are also present on the page

fields.name: save-and-continue
fields.label: Save and continue

fields.name: change-link
fields.label: Change
fields.notes: Per-row/per-section 'Change' link. The page object exposes named change links by id: #country-of-origin-change-link, #goods-movement-services-change-link, #transporter-contacts-change-link, #transport-to-bip-change-link, #traders-change-link, #organisation-change-link, #responsible-person-contact-details-change-link, #commodity-change-bulk-link-0. ched-p-manipulation.spec.ts asserts the review page renders AT LEAST 14 Change links (expect(changeLinksCount).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(14)).

fields.name: add-contact-address
fields.label: Add the contact address for consignment
fields.conditionalOn: shown only when no contact address for consignment has been entered yet (variant-only branch; in the corroborated trace the contact address block was already populated so the populated block rendered instead of this add link)
fields.evidence: page-objects/notification/ReviewNotificationPage.ts:157-159 (linkAddContactAddress getByRole('link',{name:'Add the contact address for consignment'})); driven at workflows/notification/ched-p-workflows.ts:1342

fields.name: amend
fields.label: Amend
fields.conditionalOn: post-submission / amend flow — not present on the pre-submission draft review captured in the trace
fields.notes: The review page is reused after submission to view/amend a notification; an 'Amend' button appears in that mode.

fields.name: review-and-submit
fields.label: Review and submit
fields.conditionalOn: hub/amend variant — a link used to enter the review page from the notification hub after amendments

fields.name: split-consignment
fields.label: Split consignment
fields.conditionalOn: variant-only — a 'Split consignment' link exists on the review page (splits commodities across notifications); never rendered in the corroborated trace
fields.notes: Related page-object helper txtCommodityMessage matches /commodities split into -/i, indicating a split-consignment summary message can render on this page.

fields.name: copy-reference
fields.label: Copy
fields.notes: Copy-to-clipboard buttons beside CHED reference, Reference for your customs declaration, and Customs document code. Custom client-side clipboard JS (see nonStandardPatterns copy-button/link-button).
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 170 (refs e71, e76, e81)

assertedReviewValues.notes: Test asserts the reference-number value renders on review (toHaveText(referenceNumber)). In the corroborated trace this field rendered empty — kept as a discrepancy in openQuestions.
Trace confirmed 'Medium risk'; tests additionally assert 'High risk' (ched-p-notification.spec.ts:48,99) and 'Low risk' (ched-p-manipulation.spec.ts:68,81).
Trace confirmed 'TILBURY (GBTIL)'; test asserts the value contains 'GBMAN' (ched-p-manipulation.spec.ts:69,82).

assertedReviewValues.locator: table with columnheader 'Catch certificate reference', row filtered by reference
assertedReviewValues.notes: Test asserts EACH uploaded catch-certificate row contains its reference, its flag state ('France'), and its attachment filename (matching /example(\(\d+\))?\.png/). Corroborates the trace's confirmed columns (reference / flag state / date of issue / attachments) and the 2-certificate multi-row behaviour.

openQuestions: This is a check-your-answers / review page with NO data-entry controls — the only interactive form control is the 'Save and continue' button; all other interactivity is Copy buttons, Change links and attachment download links. Recorded 'fields' accordingly.

The 'IUU exemptions' section (H3, 'No exemptions specified' when none) is the IUU-specific block on this page — in this trace no exemptions were entered so the populated-state copy/table is a gap (never exercised here). Needs an error/populated trace to capture the exemption row format.

The catch-certificate summary table columns are confirmed: 'Catch certificate reference', 'Flag state of catching vessel', 'Date of issue', 'Attachments'. Each certificate additionally renders its own H3 'Reference: <ref>' and a commodity table (Commodity code | Species, description). Two certificates rendered here (1 of 2, 2 of 2) proving the multi-certificate assertion.

This is a CHED-P/fish trace, so the page carries POAO/health-certificate, approved-establishment, traders and transport review sections. For the standalone IUU rebuild it is a gap which of these sections carry over — the IUU-relevant blocks are the catch-certificate tables, IUU exemptions, and the reference/customs-code block; the approved-establishment and full POAO trader/transport review may not all apply.
Region of origin code and Consignment reference number rendered empty in this trace — confirms the fields exist on the review but not their populated format.
The 'Not Submitted' status and 'Last updated ... by ...' audit line are review-page furniture; whether the rebuild surfaces a status/audit line is a product decision (gap).

IUU exemptions populated-state remains a GAP: the entire QA automation repo (page-objects, workflows, tests) contains NO reference to 'exemption' — grep -rin 'exemption' returns nothing. So neither trace nor test exercises an entered exemption. The 'No exemptions specified' empty-state is confirmed from the trace only; the populated exemption row format is unknown and is a question for a human.

DISCREPANCY (finding, not a correction): the test ched-p-notification.spec.ts:47 asserts the Consignment reference number (#reference-number) renders with a value on review, but the corroborated trace rendered this field EMPTY. Trace (rendered reality) wins and stays confirmed-empty; the disagreement is recorded here. Likely the trace's notification had no consignment reference entered while the test's did — needs a human to confirm the field is optional.

Change-link count is a stated rule: ched-p-manipulation.spec.ts:63-64 asserts the review page renders AT LEAST 14 'Change' links. For the standalone IUU rebuild the exact set of editable sections (and therefore change links) will differ — this is the CHED-P/fish count, an upper-bound reference not an IUU requirement.

Page-object exposes review-page controls never rendered in the corroborated trace, added as inferred fields: 'Amend' button (post-submission amend mode), 'Review and submit' link (hub/amend entry), 'Split consignment' link + 'commodities split into -' message (split-consignment variant), and 'Add the contact address for consignment' link (shown only when no contact address entered yet). Which of these carry into the standalone IUU journey is a product decision (gap) — IUU is a new simple app and may not have amend/split flows.

Named change-link ids in the page object (#country-of-origin-change-link, #goods-movement-services-change-link, #transporter-contacts-change-link, #transport-to-bip-change-link, #traders-change-link, #organisation-change-link, #responsible-person-contact-details-change-link, #commodity-change-bulk-link-0) reveal the editable review sections IPAFFS groups by. These are POAO/transport-oriented; the IUU-relevant editable sections are the catch-certificate details, commodity, and contact — the transporter/traders/goods-movement change targets may not all apply to the rebuilt IUU journey (gap).
